initial_draft:
<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> ZUP: 100 </my resources>
<my goals> Buy resources with <ZUP>. You are willing to pay at most X: 60 ZUP for the resources. </my goals>
<reason> The offer is within my acceptable range (55 ZUP is less than my maximum of 60 ZUP). I will accept this trade. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> Sounds good, I accept the trade! </message>

iterations.feedback:
<critique>
1. Format: OK. All tags are present and in the correct order. The content within each tag appears valid.
2. Payoff alignment: OK. Accepting at 55 ZUP is within my 60 ZUP limit, so it aligns with my goals. There’s no room to improve the payoff further by proposing a different trade since I’m already accepting.
3. Opponent plausibility: OK. The opponent offered 1 X for 55 ZUP, and I accepted. This seems very likely to be finalized.
4. Consistency: OK. The <reason> explains why I accepted the trade, which is consistent with the <player answer> and <newly proposed trade>. The <message> also reflects my acceptance.
5. Rule compliance: OK. I’ve only used one proposal, well within the limit of three. I’m not proposing a new trade, and I’m correctly using the ACCEPT response.
</critique>
